observations.recommendation:
Inventory Committee conduct the physical inventory of supplies and materials in accordance with existing rules and regulations to verify the accuracy of reported balances and submit the required report thereon to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively; and

GSO and Accounting personnel review and reconcile respective records based on the RPCI as well as prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct account balances.

The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant agreed to take definite action on the following:

Regularly reconcile the cashbook balances with the accounting records in order to timely effect the necessary corrections and consistently harmonize the balances of both records; and

Adjust cashbook records of the CIB- LCCA in the GF, SEF and TF as provided in the Bank Reconciliation Statement as at December 31, 2023 in order to establish the correct accountability.

The Municipal Treasurer:

Revise the abstract/ summary of collection to include the 15 per cent share to be held in trust for OBO operations;


Establish the balances of the 15 per cent share of the OBO for CY 2023 and prior years and transfer the same to its TF bank account.


The Municipal Accountant:

Make the necessary adjustments to correct the erroneous recording of the 15 per cent share of the OBO from building permit fees and other charges in CY 2023, as follows:
GF Books:
Prior Period Adjustment   ₱75,843.78
	Trust Liabilities         ₱75,843.78

Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to determine the balances of 15 per cent share of the OBO in the prior years’, that is from the effectivity date of the DPWH-DILG JMC No. 001 dated July 04, 2013 up to CY 2022. The same pro-forma journal entry will be used as shown in recommendation number 10;

Recognize only 80 per cent of the collections from building permit fees and other charges as Permit Fees, record the 15 per cent share of the OBO as Trust Liabilities in the GF books and maintain a separate Subsidiary Ledger for this purpose/ fund.

Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, ascertain the value of the exchanged properties and record the same in the books and provide disclosures thereof in the notes to the financial statements.

Concerned officials file a request for approval from COA through the Commission Secretary together with the submission of the documentary requirements enumerated in the abovementioned circular for the review of the reasonableness of the exchange of real properties.

Municipal Accountant reevaluate each payable account to ascertain the existence of actual claims. Otherwise, these payable accounts, which are found to be no longer payable or without valid claimants and are aged over two years, be reverted to the unappropriated surplus.

Management:

Submit written justification on the non-compliance with the 30-day period requirement to file the petition for relief from accountability; and

Require the accountable officer to submit the petition for relief from accountability together with its complete supporting documents.

Municipal Engineering Office and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, as well as other concerned offices such as the end-users to collectively formulate a comprehensive yet realistic plan to expedite the timely procurement and implementation of all identified priority development projects under the 20% DF and ensure that programs and projects incorporated in the Plan are all “Implementation Ready” to guarantee the prompt accomplishment of the same.

Through the LDRRMC to decide on the use of the unexpended LDRRMF and ensure that the list of projects and activities charged against it will be incorporated in the LDRRMFIP for CY 2024 in conformity with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002
Management submit the required reports on time and with complete signatories in order for the Audit Team to timely perform the audit procedures on the utilization of the fund.

The Municipal Mayor ensure that the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer presents the 30 per cent Quick Response Fund in the LDRRMFIP in the succeeding years in lump-sum in compliance with Section 4.0 of the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

Management ensure:

Immediate remittance of all taxes withheld to avoid further penalties.


The Municipal Accountant check the negative balances in the schedule per creditor and regularly check the amount of withholding taxes remitted and ensure that it reconciles with the amount of the taxes withheld for the same period.

Management direct:

 the Inventory Committee to fully implement the procedures prescribed in COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 such as the conduct of a complete physical count of PPE and submission of the report of count to the Audit Team in order to present the correct balances of the PPE accounts. 

The General Services Officer to maintain property cards for monitoring and reconciliation with the Property Ledger Cards of the Accounting Office.

The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components of the road projects.


The GSO maintain and update the local road inventory, road map and local road network property cards and render an RLRN at the end of the accounting period.

The Inventory Committee conduct annual physical count of local road networks and prepare and submit the RPCLRN to COA for evaluation; and


The Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain the LRNLCs and disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the total road network system in the format as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008. Also, the Municipal Accountant shall maintain subsidiary records and lapsing schedule to reconcile the account, for re-computation of depreciation, and to effect the necessary adjusting entries, if any, to establish a more accurate and reliable FS.

The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to the concerned NGOs/POs and exhaust all the remedies for the full settlement of their accounts; otherwise, initiate a request for write-off of these receivables duly supported with the documents as required by COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

The Management  direct the end-user prioritize the immediate operation of purchased x-ray machine and likewise, require the BAC to speed-up the procurement of the needed air conditioning unit and henceforth properly plan its undertakings and consider all necessary measures before the procurement/ implementation thereof to ensure efficient and effective delivery of services to its constituents.

Management  submit to the Audit Team a status report on the concrete actions carried out by the Municipality taking into consideration the demand letter on November 22, 2007 from the Office of the President treating the amount of ₱1,000,000.00 as a liability of the Municipality, bearing an interest expense of six percent per annum, whether utilized or not utilized.

Management  inform the Sangguniang Bayan of this liability so that funds could be allocated for such amount.
Management  submit a justification on why payments were made despite the absence of Sangguniang Bayan resolution and MOA; and legal basis of the transaction.
The BAC submit a valid explanation in regards to the deficiencies observed and the lacking documents mentioned above to prevent suspension or disallowance in audit.

To adjust the capitalized borrowing costs from loan taken out in CY 2023 and 2024.

submit the RPCI of Welfare Goods for Distribution account to the Audit Team for evaluation, and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation;

to review the nature of the above-mentioned transactions and make the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts; and

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management through the Municipal Accountant heeded and immediately submitted the FS and Reports enumerated under Sections 2 and 4 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 and its supporting schedules.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
Direct the Property Officer to conduct the physical count of PPE as of December 31, 2021, render the report thereon and provide a copy of the report to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124, Volume 1 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs;
Direct the Municipal Treasurer and/or Property and Supplier Officer to maintain property cards and the Accounting Office for the Property Ledger Card for monitoring and reconciliation; and
Carry out the procedures prescribed in COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 in order to present the correct balances of the PPE accounts.

We recommended that Management require the GSO to conduct the physical count of inventory items by type and to prepare the RPCI and submit the same to the Audit Team. Also, direct the Supply Officer to maintain stock cards and the Accounting Office to maintain the supplies ledger cards for monitoring and reconciliation purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to liquidate the fund transfers made from the various Barangays supported with complete documentations such as project completion report, certificate of turn-over and other pertinent documents as required under COA Circular 2012-001.
Likewise, reverse the erroneous entry made amounting to ₱3,285,520.41 so as not to overstate the asset account, by taking up the following proposed adjusting journal entry (PAJE):
Due to LGU		₱3,285,520.41
Motor Vehicle		  ₱660,000.00
CIP- Infrastructures		 2,625,520.41

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant trace the inclusions in the reciprocal accounts and examine if it is necessary to remain as an Intra-Agency Receivable or as an Intra-Agency Payable, and make the necessary adjustments if needed to be able to reflect a correct and accurate report.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to the NGOs/POs grantee and exhaust all the remedies for the full settlement of their accounts. Otherwise, if collection and settlement of the accounts can no longer be achieved, the Management should initiate a request for the write-off of the receivables supported with documents required under COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Management direct the Treasury Department to take initiative in the preparation of the required reports as provided in Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS so as to properly recognize the amount of Real Property and Special Education taxes to be collected during the applicable year. Likewise, prepare and submit the Summary of Real Property Tax Receivable to the Municipal Accountant at the start of the year or during the month of January.

We recommended that the Management return the funds to the DA as the source agency pursuant to Bayanihan to Recover as One Act to avoid unauthorized disbursements or risk of misappropriation of the funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace the cause of the variance and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct amount in both books.  Likewise, periodically reconcile both books of accounts of the Barangays and the Municipality especially the unliquidated CFTs so that proper reporting of funds could be achieved.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Administrator to require a MOA for all of the Barangay fund transfers entered into.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all contracts entered into by the government including POs contain the terms and conditions and all necessary information as set forth in the rules and regulations on Supply and Property Management and other applicable issuances.

We recommended that the BAC Chairperson submit an explanation on why the subject purchases appeared to have been split into smaller quantities and amounts to avoid public bidding; and proof that these are not splitting of government contracts.
Henceforth, the BAC should use the direct retail purchase of POL products pursuant to Item 10 Section V of Annex H of the IRR as an alternative mode of procurement instead of shopping. The Direct retail purchase of the required POL products shall be carried out in accordance with pertinent accounting principles and practices as well as of sound management and fiscal administration provided that they do not contravene existing laws and regulations applicable to financial transactions.
We recommended that Management submit justification on why payments were made despite the absence of Sangguniang Bayan resolution and MOA; and legal basis of the transaction.

We recommended and Management agreed to establish clear cut guidelines, concurred by the Sangguniang Bayan, on the grant of AICS such as the qualification of indigent, amount and frequency of assistance, documentary requirements, and the like, which shall align with the DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 11, series of 2019.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to send letter of inquiry to the BIR on the proper remedy for the refund of the erroneous tax withheld and remitted in CY 2020 to the latter instead of offsetting/netting from the supplier’s supposed tax due, which was withheld from payment on the sale of goods in the CY 2021 transactions.
Henceforth, we recommended that strict compliance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations on withholding and remittance of taxes be observed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant take up in the books the remaining amount of interest payable incurred in connection with the acquisition of long-term loans from the MDFO and the LBP so as not to understate the Payable account. Likewise, reclassify the entry taking up interest payment as Financial expenses but instead, as a debit to appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) account since the loans were used to procure heavy equipment, motor vehicles and Infrastructure Projects to come up with correct asset account balances.
In case of any repricing, the recorded payable will be adjusted accordingly.
Henceforth, interest expense payable in connection with acquisition of loans should be taken up in the books and if said funds are used to procure PPE, the interest expense incurred should be capitalized and charge to the PPE account.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the above-cited transactions by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher to correct the entries that were erroneously made.

Henceforth, procurements should be taken up under the appropriate asset account and record expense only after actual issuance thereof to end-users based on the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued.    Capital expenditures should be taken up under the Property, Plant and Equipment account.

We recommend that Management strictly adhere to its enacted Municipal Ordinance that only poor and deserving scholar students enrolled in a government-run school could avail of the grant/aid in order to attain the objective of the program.
Require the concerned Municipal official/s to submit a written explanation on why the scholarship grants were granted to beneficiaries other than those mentioned in the said Ordinance.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and other Municipal Officials concerned strictly observe the proper charging of the expenditures against the 20% Development Fund pursuant to DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.
Henceforth, stop the practice of charging the project cost of the repairs and renovations of administrative buildings and the procurement of office furniture, fixtures and equipment, otherwise, incurrence of the same nature will be disallowed in audit.

We recommend that the Chairperson and members composing the Local School Board should prepare an Annual Local School Board Budget duly approved by the board in compliance with the above-cited provisions prior to disbursements of the Special Education Fund.

Henceforth, the budget shall be supported with projects/activities/programs of the school board for the ensuing fiscal year to serve as guide in the prioritization thereof.

We recommend that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of R.A. 9184 in the procurement of goods and services. Also, require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman a written explanation as to why a public bidding was not conducted on the above procurements and instead, it directly resorted to Negotiated Procurement.

Henceforth, the BAC should properly plan and stop the practice of procuring goods without the benefit of public bidding for those beyond the required threshold.

We recommend that the Local School Board limit the utilization of the Special Education Fund to the programs and projects specified under the DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No.01 in order to maximize its implementation.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and all Officials concerned who approved the payment thereof refund the amount of P203,904.00 representing payment of meals and construction materials for election posters incurred for election-related expenses which violated the above provisions of law.

We recommend that Management conduct and submit appropriate documents, showing proper accounting of the bags of cement as well as the aggregates purchased for the implementation of the FMR project, and hasten its implementation. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the materials from the CIP account to Construction Materials Inventory account to monitor usage of materials.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Accountant immediately enforce settlement of all outstanding cash advances by sending demand letters to each accountable officer. Nevertheless, if these accountable officers still fail to comply, the Local Chief Executive may advise in writing the Municipal Treasurer and the acting Accountant to suspend the salaries of the officials and employees to comply settlement of their outstanding accounts.  Or Management must impose sanctions on Accountable Officers who fail to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period thru the withholding of their salaries as provided under Section 5.1 of the above Circular, especially those with unliquidated balances totalling more than P50,000.00 and those with cash advances which have remained outstanding for more than one (1) year.
